# Dual progress bars for file operations ## Why The `TransferProgressDialog` shows one byte-based progress bar with text stats below. But file operations have two independent progress dimensions: bytes transferred and files processed. These can diverge significantly — copying one huge file vs many tiny files produces very different bar behaviors. Showing both gives the user a clearer mental model of what's happening (design principle: radical transparency). The progress bar code is also duplicated — `TransferProgressDialog` and `ProgressOverlay` each have their own inline CSS bars with no shared component. ## What we're building 1. A reusable `ProgressBar.svelte` component (pure visual bar, no layout opinions) 2. Dual progress bars in `TransferProgressDialog` (size + file count) 3. `ProgressOverlay` refactored to use the shared component ## What we're NOT building **Rollback progress.** The backend's `CopyTransaction::rollback()` (`state.rs:381`) is synchronous with no event emission. Backwards-animating bars would require backend changes (emitting progress during file deletion). The current spinner + "Deleting N copied files..." is correct for an indeterminate operation. Future work if needed. ## Design decisions ### ProgressBar is just the bar **Why:** `ProgressOverlay` uses a horizontal row layout (bar + percentage + ETA inline). `TransferProgressDialog` needs a grid layout (label, bar, detail). These are fundamentally different layouts. If the component includes label/detail props, one consumer won't use them, making the API dishonest. A pure bar component is more composable — consumers arrange labels however they need. ### Size variants instead of pixel props **Why:** The codebase uses design tokens (`--spacing-*`, `--radius-*`, `--font-size-*`) rather than raw values. A `size: 'sm' | 'md'` prop (4px / 8px) is idiomatic. `sm` for `ProgressOverlay`'s compact floating indicator, `md` for dialog-level progress. ### Inline grid layout for dual bars **Why:** A vertical stack of label-above-bar pairs creates a text/bar/text/bar/text sandwich — visually noisy. An inline grid aligns both bars side by side with labels left and stats right. Compact, scannable, bars are the focal element: ``` Size [========================] 1.2 / 3.5 GB Files [================== ] 42 / 100 45.2 MB/s · ~2 min remaining ``` CSS grid with `grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto`. Meta row spans all columns. ## Implementation ### Step 1: Create `ProgressBar.svelte` **File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressBar.svelte` Props: - `value: number` — 0–1 fractional progress (required) - `size?: 'sm' | 'md'` — bar height. `sm` = 4px, `md` = 8px. Default `'md'` - `fillColor?: string` — CSS color override. Default `var(--color-accent)` - `ariaLabel?: string` — accessible label for screen readers Internals: - `percent = $derived(Math.min(100, Math.round(value * 100)))` - Track div: `role="progressbar"`, `aria-valuenow={percent}`, `aria-valuemin={0}`, `aria-valuemax={100}` - Fill div: `transition: width 0.15s ease-out` - All styling uses existing tokens (`--color-bg-tertiary`, `--color-accent`, `--radius-xs`) ### Step 2: Refactor `ProgressOverlay.svelte` **File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressOverlay.svelte` **Intent:** Validate the new component in the simpler consumer first. - Import and use `` - Remove the inline `.progress-bar` and `.progress-fill` CSS rules - Keep `.progress-row`, `.progress-text`, `.progress-eta` (the surrounding layout is unchanged) ### Step 3: Dual bars in `TransferProgressDialog.svelte` **File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/transfer/TransferProgressDialog.svelte` Script: - Import `ProgressBar` - Add: `const filePercentComplete = $derived(filesTotal > 0 ? (filesDone / filesTotal) * 100 : 0)` Template — replace lines 799–828 (the progress section + stats section) with a CSS grid: ```svelte
Size 0 ? bytesDone / bytesTotal : 0} /> {formatBytes(bytesDone)} / {formatBytes(bytesTotal)} {operationType === 'trash' ? 'Items' : 'Files'} 0 ? filesDone / filesTotal : 0} /> {filesDone} / {filesTotal}
{#if stats.bytesPerSecond > 0}{formatBytes(stats.bytesPerSecond)}/s{/if} {#if stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining !== null}~{formatDuration(stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining)} remaining{/if}
``` CSS: - `.progress-grid`: `display: grid; grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto; gap: var(--spacing-xs) var(--spacing-sm); align-items: center; padding: 0 var(--spacing-xl); margin-bottom: var(--spacing-md)` - `.progress-label`: `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-text-tertiary)` - `.progress-detail`: `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-text-secondary); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; text-align: right` - `.progress-meta`: `grid-column: 1 / -1; display: flex; justify-content: space-between; font-size: var(--font-size-sm)` - `.progress-speed`: `color: var(--color-text-secondary); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` - `.progress-eta`: `color: var(--color-text-tertiary)` - Remove old: `.progress-bar-container`, `.progress-bar`, `.progress-info`, `.progress-percent`, `.eta`, `.stats-section`, `.stat-row`, `.stat-label`, `.stat-value` ### Step 4: Update docs - `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/CLAUDE.md` — add ProgressBar section with props table - `apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/CLAUDE.md` — update "Progress bar with ETA" → "Dual progress bars (size + file count) with ETA" ### Step 5: Checks - `./scripts/check.sh --svelte` (typecheck, lint, stylelint, format) ## Testing - Manual: `pnpm dev` → copy a large folder → verify two bars animate independently - Manual: delete files → both bars progress - Manual: trash files → "Items" label appears instead of "Files" - Manual: cancel a copy → rollback spinner still works unchanged - Manual: trigger indexing → ProgressOverlay bar still works - Automated: existing vitest + checks should pass (no behavior changes, just UI restructuring)

1→# Design principles
     2→
     3→- Prefer an elegant architecture over quick hacks. We have time to do outstanding work, and we are in this for the long
     4→  run.
     5→- **Platform-native, not generic.** The app should look and feel as if it was specifically made for the user's OS. Never
     6→  generalize user-facing text, labels, or behavior to be "cross-platform" — instead, fork by OS. On macOS, say "Finder",
     7→  "Trash", "System Settings". On Linux, say "file manager", "Trash" (FreeDesktop spec), and use DE-specific terminology
     8→  where possible. Windows (later) gets its own native terms too. This applies to error messages, menu labels, tooltips,
     9→  and any user-visible string. Use `isMacOS()` / `cfg(target_os)` to branch — a few extra lines of platform-specific
    10→  text are always better than one watered-down generic string.
    11→- Always apply radical transparency: make the internals of what's happening available. Like, don't just put a "Syncing"
    12→  spinner but write exactly what's happening. Don't overshare/overcomplicate, but the user must understand what's
    13→  happening to an extent that they could explain it to someone else if asked.
    14→- Always make features extremely user-friendly. The UI should help the user accomplish their goals with minimal
    15→  friction.
    16→- This is a keyboard-first app. Everything must work with the mouse, too, but we should make it easy and straightforward
    17→  to use all features with the keyboard.
    18→- When shortcuts are available for a feature, always display the shortcut in a tooltip or somewhere less prominent than
    19→  the main UI.
    20→- For longer processes:
    21→    1. Always run the process in the background. Blocking the UI or other actions is an absolute no-go.
    22→    2. Show some anim to communicate that the app is doing something.
    23→    3. If we know what the end state looks like and we can quantify the progress, show a progress bar and counter.
    24→    4. If we have a guess how long the operation will take, show an ETA.
    25→    5. Progress bars staying longer at 100% than at 99% or any other percentage is NOT allowed. If we're done with the
    26→       part of an operation that we could quantify and displayed a progress bar for it but we have something else to do
    27→       (e.g. we loaded the data and now we're making calculations on the data), then it's a new state, show another
    28→       state!
    29→- All actions longer than ~1 second should be immediately cancelable, canceling not just the UI but any background
    30→  processes as well, to avoid wasting the user's resources. If rolling back is an option, we should consider that too.
    31→- Always keep accessibility in mind. Features should be available to people with impaired vision, hearing, and cognitive
    32→  disabilities.
    33→
    34→For concrete tokens and component patterns, see [design-system.md](design-system.md).

content.text:
This is important. For trash, `itemSizes` can be `undefined` when not all items have size data (line 298). When `itemSizes` is `undefined`, the backend sets `bytes_total = 0` and `bytes_done = 0`. The plan's Size bar would show "0 B / 0 B (NaN%)" -- wait, let me check the math: `bytesTotal > 0 ? (bytesDone / bytesTotal) * 100 : 0` gives 0, and `formatBytes(0) / formatBytes(0)` gives "0 B / 0 B (0%)". That's not great but not NaN.

However, there's a more fundamental issue: for trash operations without `itemSizes`, the Size bar will show `0 B / 0 B (0%)` and stay at 0% forever, even as files are being trashed. That's misleading.

Now I have all the information needed for my review.

---

Here is my numbered list of findings:

**1. `percentComplete` becomes dead code and is not mentioned for removal.**

The plan replaces the only two usages of `percentComplete` (lines 802, 805) with inline expressions in the new template. But the `$derived` declaration at line 189 (`const percentComplete = $derived(...)`) is not listed for removal. It should be removed to avoid leaving dead code in the script block. This is minor but worth calling out so the implementer doesn't forget.

**2. Trash without `itemSizes` will show a misleading Size bar stuck at "0 B / 0 B (0%)".**

When trashing files where not all items have known sizes (see `dialog-state.svelte.ts` line 298 -- `itemSizes` is set to `undefined` if any item lacks size data), the backend sets `bytes_total = 0` and `bytes_done = 0` for the entire operation. The plan's Size bar would render as a permanently-empty bar with "0 B / 0 B (0%)". This is worse than the current UI, which simply omits the size row when data is unavailable. The plan should either: (a) hide the Size row when `bytesTotal === 0` (and the operation is not in scanning phase), or (b) note this edge case so the implementer handles it. This is the most significant gap in the plan.

**3. The `currentFile` indicator placement relative to the scanning guard needs clarification.**

The plan says "preserve the current file indicator after the grid" and the code snippet places `currentFile` outside the `{#if phase !== 'scanning'}` block. That is correct -- during scanning, the backend does emit `current_file` in scan progress events (`scan.rs` line 433), and showing the currently-scanned file during scanning is useful for radical transparency. However, the plan should explicitly note this is intentional, since a reader might assume `currentFile` should also be hidden during scanning.

**4. No layout-jump mitigation when the grid appears.**

When the phase transitions from `scanning` to the active phase, the progress grid suddenly appears (two bars + meta row), which shifts the `currentFile` indicator and action buttons downward. In the current UI this is less noticeable because the progress section is always rendered (just empty). The plan's `{#if phase !== 'scanning'}` will cause a visible layout jump. Consider using `min-height` on the grid's parent container, or rendering the grid with `visibility: hidden` during scanning, to reserve space. Whether this matters depends on how fast the scanning phase typically is -- for small operations it may be instantaneous, but for large directory trees it could last several seconds.

**5. No `ariaLabel` specified for the two ProgressBar instances in the plan's template.**

The plan defines `ariaLabel` as an optional prop on `ProgressBar` and calls out accessibility as an improvement (issue 7). But the template snippet on lines 110 and 117 does not pass `ariaLabel` to either `<ProgressBar>`. Without it, screen readers will announce the progress bar with no label context. The bars should get something like `ariaLabel="Size progress"` and `ariaLabel="File progress"` respectively. This is especially important since the plan highlights the accessibility improvement as a feature.

**6. The `stats` object reference may not match the plan's usage.**

The plan's template references `stats.bytesPerSecond` and `stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining` (lines 121, 124). Looking at the actual code, `stats` is a `$derived` object at line ~192 that computes `bytesPerSecond` and `estimatedSecondsRemaining`. This checks out and is correct.

**7. Delete operations: the "Size" bar label may be confusing.**

For delete operations, bytes represent the size of files being deleted, not data being transferred. The label "Size" is technically accurate but could confuse users who expect "Size" to mean transfer size. This is a minor UX concern and the current UI already shows "Size:" as a stat-row label for delete, so there is precedent. No action needed, but worth noting.

**8. The plan is solid overall.**

The grid CSS approach (`auto 1fr auto` columns) is robust and will handle varying label/detail widths well. The `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` on the detail column prevents number-width jitter. The meta row spanning all columns with flex justify-content between speed and ETA is clean. The component extraction (ProgressBar as a pure bar) is well-reasoned and the two-consumer validation approach (ProgressOverlay first, then TransferProgressDialog) is good sequencing.

**Summary of items requiring action before implementation:**

- **Issue 2 is the most important** -- the Size bar will be broken for trash operations without size data. This needs a conditional hide or fallback.
- **Issue 5** should be fixed in the plan template (add `ariaLabel` props).
- **Issue 1** is trivial but should be noted (remove `percentComplete`).
- **Issue 4** is a judgment call depending on how long scanning typically takes. Worth acknowledging.
- **Issue 3** is just a documentation clarity point.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/Users/veszelovszki/projects-git/vdavid/cmdr/apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/transfer/TransferProgressDialog.svelte`
- `/Users/veszelovszki/projects-git/vdavid/cmdr/apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressOverlay.svelte`
- `/Users/veszelovszki/projects-git/vdavid/cmdr/apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/file_system/write_operations/trash.rs`
- `/Users/veszelovszki/projects-git/vdavid/cmdr/apps/desktop/src/lib/file-explorer/pane/dialog-state.svelte.ts`
- `/Users/veszelovszki/projects-git/vdavid/cmdr/apps/desktop/src/app.css`

3. Is the grid CSS robust (column sizing, alignment, responsive behavior in the dialog)?
4. Anything else that would prevent clean implementation?

Return a numbered list of findings. If the plan is solid, say so explicitly.

input.old_string:
### Hide progress grid during scanning phase

During scanning, both `bytesTotal` and `filesTotal` are 0. Two empty bars at 0% with "0 / 0" labels is ugly and
uninformative. The stage indicator already communicates "Scanning" with a spinner. The progress grid is only shown once
the active phase begins (when `phase !== 'scanning'`).
input.new_string:
### Hide progress grid during scanning phase

During scanning, both `bytesTotal` and `filesTotal` are 0. Two empty bars at 0% with "0 / 0" labels is ugly and
uninformative. The stage indicator already communicates "Scanning" with a spinner. The progress grid is only shown once
the active phase begins (when `phase !== 'scanning'`).

### Hide size bar when byte totals are unavailable

at "0 B / 0 B (0%)" is misleading. Conditionally hide the size row when `bytesTotal === 0` and we're past scanning.

# Dual progress bars for file operations ## Why The `TransferProgressDialog` shows one byte-based progress bar with text stats below. But file operations have two independent progress dimensions: bytes transferred and files processed. These can diverge significantly — copying one huge file vs many tiny files produces very different bar behaviors. Showing both gives the user a clearer mental model of what's happening (design principle: radical transparency). The progress bar code is also duplicated — `TransferProgressDialog` and `ProgressOverlay` each have their own inline CSS bars with no shared component. ## What we're building 1. A reusable `ProgressBar.svelte` component (pure visual bar, no layout opinions) 2. Dual progress bars in `TransferProgressDialog` (size + file count) 3. `ProgressOverlay` refactored to use the shared component ## What we're NOT building **Rollback progress.** The backend's `CopyTransaction::rollback()` (`state.rs:381`) is synchronous with no event emission. Backwards-animating bars would require backend changes (emitting progress during file deletion). The current spinner + "Deleting N copied files..." is correct for an indeterminate operation. Future work if needed. ## Design decisions ### ProgressBar is just the bar **Why:** `ProgressOverlay` uses a horizontal row layout (bar + percentage + ETA inline). `TransferProgressDialog` needs a grid layout (label, bar, detail). These are fundamentally different layouts. If the component includes label/detail props, one consumer won't use them, making the API dishonest. A pure bar component is more composable — consumers arrange labels however they need. ### Size variants instead of pixel props **Why:** The codebase uses design tokens (`--spacing-*`, `--radius-*`, `--font-size-*`) rather than raw values. A `size: 'sm' | 'md'` prop (4px / 8px) is idiomatic. `sm` for `ProgressOverlay`'s compact floating indicator, `md` for dialog-level progress. Border radius scales with size: `sm` uses `--radius-xs` (2px), `md` uses `--radius-sm` (4px) — matching what each consumer had before. ### No fillColor prop The only potential use was rollback styling, which we're not building. Removing it keeps the API minimal and avoids an escape hatch that bypasses the design token system. Can be added later if a real use case arises. ### Inline grid layout for dual bars **Why:** A vertical stack of label-above-bar pairs creates a text/bar/text/bar/text sandwich — visually noisy. An inline grid aligns both bars side by side with labels left and stats right. Compact, scannable, bars are the focal element: ``` Size [========================] 1.2 / 3.5 GB (34%) Files [================== ] 42 / 100 45.2 MB/s · ~2 min remaining ``` CSS grid with `grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto`. Meta row spans all columns. ### Hide progress grid during scanning phase During scanning, both `bytesTotal` and `filesTotal` are 0. Two empty bars at 0% with "0 / 0" labels is ugly and uninformative. The stage indicator already communicates "Scanning" with a spinner. The progress grid is only shown once the active phase begins (when `phase !== 'scanning'`). ## Implementation ### Step 1: Create `ProgressBar.svelte` **File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressBar.svelte` Props: - `value: number` — 0–1 fractional progress (required) - `size?: 'sm' | 'md'` — bar height + radius. `sm` = 4px / `--radius-xs`, `md` = 8px / `--radius-sm`. Default `'md'` - `ariaLabel?: string` — accessible label for screen readers Internals: - `percent = $derived(Math.min(100, Math.round(value * 100)))` - Track div: `role="progressbar"`, `aria-valuenow={percent}`, `aria-valuemin={0}`, `aria-valuemax={100}`, optional `aria-label={ariaLabel}` - Fill div: `transition: width 0.15s ease-out` (standardized from the existing 0.1s and 0.3s) - Styling: `--color-bg-tertiary` track, `--color-accent` fill Note: This also improves accessibility — the existing `TransferProgressDialog` bar had no `role="progressbar"` or ARIA attributes. ### Step 2: Refactor `ProgressOverlay.svelte` **File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressOverlay.svelte` **Intent:** Validate the new component in the simpler consumer first. - Import and use `` - Remove the inline `.progress-bar` and `.progress-fill` CSS rules - Keep `.progress-row`, `.progress-text`, `.progress-eta` (the surrounding layout is unchanged) ### Step 3: Dual bars in `TransferProgressDialog.svelte` **File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/transfer/TransferProgressDialog.svelte` Script: - Import `ProgressBar` Template — replace lines 799–828 (progress section + stats section) with a CSS grid. Preserve the current file indicator (lines 830–835) after the grid. Only show the grid when past the scanning phase: ```svelte {#if phase !== 'scanning'}
Size 0 ? bytesDone / bytesTotal : 0} /> {formatBytes(bytesDone)} / {formatBytes(bytesTotal)} ({Math.round(bytesTotal > 0 ? (bytesDone / bytesTotal) * 100 : 0)}%) {operationType === 'trash' ? 'Items' : 'Files'} 0 ? filesDone / filesTotal : 0} /> {filesDone} / {filesTotal}
{#if stats.bytesPerSecond > 0} {formatBytes(stats.bytesPerSecond)}/s {/if} {#if stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining !== null} ~{formatDuration(stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining)} remaining {/if}
{/if} ``` CSS: - `.progress-grid`: `display: grid; grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto; gap: var(--spacing-xs) var(--spacing-sm); align-items: center; padding: 0 var(--spacing-xl); margin-bottom: var(--spacing-md)` - `.progress-label`: `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-text-tertiary)` - `.progress-detail`: `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-text-secondary); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; text-align: right` - `.progress-meta`: `grid-column: 1 / -1; display: flex; justify-content: space-between; font-size: var(--font-size-sm)` - `.progress-speed`: `color: var(--color-text-secondary); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` - `.progress-eta`: `color: var(--color-text-tertiary)` - Remove old: `.progress-section`, `.progress-bar-container`, `.progress-bar`, `.progress-info`, `.progress-percent`, `.eta`, `.stats-section`, `.stat-row`, `.stat-label`, `.stat-value` ### Step 4: Update docs **`apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/CLAUDE.md`** — add ProgressBar section following the existing format (see ProgressOverlay section for the pattern). Props table with types and notes. Mention consumers (ProgressOverlay, TransferProgressDialog). **`apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/CLAUDE.md`** — in the TransferProgressDialog bullet, update "Progress bar with ETA, speed (MB/s), current file" → "Dual progress bars (size + file count) with speed, ETA, current file" ### Step 5: Checks - `./scripts/check.sh --svelte` (typecheck, lint, stylelint, format) ## Testing - Manual: `pnpm dev` → copy a large folder → verify two bars animate independently - Manual: delete files → both bars progress (backend tracks bytes for delete/trash too) - Manual: trash files → "Items" label appears instead of "Files" - Manual: cancel a copy → rollback spinner still works unchanged - Manual: trigger indexing → ProgressOverlay bar still works - Manual: start an operation → verify scanning phase shows stages but no empty bars - Automated: existing vitest + checks should pass (no behavior changes, just UI restructuring)

I have now reviewed the plan spec thoroughly against the actual codebase. Here is my assessment.

---

## Final Review: `dual-progress-bars-plan.md`

**The plan is solid.** All references to the codebase are accurate, the design decisions are well-reasoned, and the two previous rounds of fixes have addressed the issues cleanly. I found no remaining correctness problems, edge case gaps, or implementation issues.

Here is what I verified:

### Correctness checks -- all pass

1. **`percentComplete` removal (line 189)**: The plan correctly identifies this derived on line 189 and its two consumers on lines 802 and 805. The replacement uses inline `bytesDone / bytesTotal` expressions in the template, which is correct.

2. **`phase` type and `'scanning'` value**: `WriteOperationPhase` is `'scanning' | 'copying' | 'deleting' | 'trashing'` (defined in `types.ts:344`). The `{#if phase !== 'scanning'}` guard is type-safe.

3. **`bytesTotal === 0` guard for trash**: The plan correctly identifies that `trashFiles` receives optional `itemSizes` (prop on line 75, passed on line 390). When `itemSizes` is undefined, `bytesTotal` stays 0 throughout. The `{#if bytesTotal > 0}` condition correctly hides the misleading size bar.

4. **Division by zero**: The files bar uses `filesTotal > 0 ? filesDone / filesTotal : 0`, which is safe. The size bar is inside `{#if bytesTotal > 0}`, so `bytesDone / bytesTotal` can never divide by zero.

5. **CSS classes to remove**: All listed classes (`.progress-section`, `.progress-bar-container`, `.progress-bar`, `.progress-info`, `.progress-percent`, `.eta`, `.stats-section`, `.stat-row`, `.stat-label`, `.stat-value`) exist in the current file and are used only in the section being replaced.

6. **Design tokens**: `--radius-xs` (2px), `--radius-sm` (4px), `--color-bg-tertiary`, `--color-accent` all exist in `app.css`.

7. **`currentFile` placement**: The plan correctly keeps `currentFile` outside the `{#if phase !== 'scanning'}` block, since the backend emits `currentFile` during scanning too (confirmed: `currentFile = event.currentFile` on line 260, `phase` starts as `'scanning'` on line 129).

8. **`formatBytes` and `formatDuration`**: Both are already imported (line 16-17).

9. **`check.sh` path**: `scripts/check.sh` exists.

10. **ProgressOverlay refactor**: The inline CSS to be removed (`.progress-bar`, `.progress-fill` on lines 93-106) and the layout CSS to keep (`.progress-row`, `.progress-text`, `.progress-eta`) are correctly identified.

### No remaining issues found

The plan is ready for implementation.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- /Users/veszelovszki/projects-git/vdavid/cmdr/apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/transfer/TransferProgressDialog.svelte
- /Users/veszelovszki/projects-git/vdavid/cmdr/apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressOverlay.svelte
- /Users/veszelovszki/projects-git/vdavid/cmdr/apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/CLAUDE.md
- /Users/veszelovszki/projects-git/vdavid/cmdr/apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/CLAUDE.md
- /Users/veszelovszki/projects-git/vdavid/cmdr/apps/desktop/src/app.css

input.plan:
# Plan: Dual progress bars for file operations ## Context `TransferProgressDialog` (copy/move/delete/trash) has one byte-based progress bar with text stats below. The backend already emits both `filesDone/filesTotal` and `bytesDone/bytesTotal`. We want two visual bars — size + file count. No reusable `ProgressBar` component exists — both `TransferProgressDialog` and `ProgressOverlay` have inline CSS bars. **Rollback:** Backend emits no progress during rollback (`CopyTransaction::rollback()` in `state.rs:381` is synchronous). Backwards-animating bars aren't feasible without backend work. Current spinner stays. ## Design ### `ProgressBar.svelte` — pure visual bar Minimal component. No labels, no layout opinions. ~30 lines. ```ts { value: number // 0–1 fractional progress (required) size?: 'sm' | 'md' // sm = 4px (ProgressOverlay), md = 8px (dialogs). Default 'md'. fillColor?: string // CSS color override. Default var(--color-accent). ariaLabel?: string // Accessible label for screen readers. } ``` Renders: track div (`role="progressbar"`, `aria-valuenow/min/max`) with fill div inside. ### Dual bar layout in `TransferProgressDialog` Inline table-like grid — labels left, bars center (flex), stats right: ``` Size [========================] 1.2 / 3.5 GB Files [================== ] 42 / 100 45.2 MB/s · ~2 min remaining ``` CSS grid: `grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto` with `align-items: center`. Labels and details use `font-size: var(--font-size-sm)`, `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums`. Gap between rows: `var(--spacing-xs)`. Meta row spans all three columns. For delete/trash: label says "Items" instead of "Files". ## Files ### 1. CREATE `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressBar.svelte` - `value` prop → `percent = $derived(Math.min(100, Math.round(value * 100)))` - Track: `background: var(--color-bg-tertiary)`, `border-radius: var(--radius-xs)`, `overflow: hidden` - Fill: `background: var(--color-accent)` (overridable via `fillColor`), `border-radius: var(--radius-xs)`, `transition: width 0.15s ease-out` - Height: `sm` → 4px, `md` → 8px ### 2. MODIFY `apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/transfer/TransferProgressDialog.svelte` **Script:** - Import `ProgressBar` - Add `filePercentComplete` derived **Template (replace lines 799–828):** ```svelte
Size 0 ? bytesDone / bytesTotal : 0} /> {formatBytes(bytesDone)} / {formatBytes(bytesTotal)} {operationType === 'trash' ? 'Items' : 'Files'} 0 ? filesDone / filesTotal : 0} /> {filesDone} / {filesTotal}
{#if stats.bytesPerSecond > 0} {formatBytes(stats.bytesPerSecond)}/s {/if} {#if stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining !== null} ~{formatDuration(stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining)} remaining {/if}
``` **CSS:** Remove old `.progress-bar-container`, `.progress-bar`, `.progress-info`, `.progress-percent`, `.eta`, `.stats-section`, `.stat-row`, `.stat-label`, `.stat-value`. Add `.progress-grid` (CSS grid), `.progress-label`, `.progress-detail`, `.progress-meta`. ### 3. MODIFY `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressOverlay.svelte` Replace inline bar markup (lines 30–44) with ``. Remove `.progress-bar` and `.progress-fill` CSS. Keep `.progress-row`, `.progress-text`, `.progress-eta`. ### 4. UPDATE docs - `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/CLAUDE.md` — add ProgressBar section - `apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/CLAUDE.md` — mention dual progress bars ## Order 1. Create `ProgressBar.svelte` 2. Refactor `ProgressOverlay.svelte` (simpler consumer, validates component) 3. Modify `TransferProgressDialog.svelte` (dual bars, restructure stats) 4. Update CLAUDE.md files 5. `./scripts/check.sh --svelte` ## Verification - `pnpm dev` → copy a large folder → two bars animate independently - Delete/trash → both bars show progress - Cancel copy → rollback spinner still works - ProgressOverlay still works during indexing scan - `./scripts/check.sh --svelte`

content.text:
The plan at `docs/specs/dual-progress-bars-plan.md` has been through 3 rounds of Opus review and is clean. Here's the summary:

**New component:** `ProgressBar.svelte` — pure visual bar, no layout opinions. Props: `value`, `size` (sm/md), `ariaLabel`.

**Dual bars in TransferProgressDialog** — CSS grid layout:
```
Size   [========================] 1.2 / 3.5 GB (34%)
Files  [==================      ] 42 / 100
       45.2 MB/s · ~2 min remaining
```

**Key details addressed through review:**
- Size bar conditionally hidden when `bytesTotal === 0` (trash without item sizes)
- Progress grid hidden during scanning phase (no ugly "0 / 0")
- `currentFile` preserved outside the grid (shown during scanning too)
- Border radius scales with size variant (sm→radius-xs, md→radius-sm)
- ARIA labels on both bar instances
- Dead `percentComplete` derived removed
- `ProgressOverlay` refactored as the first step to validate the component

Ready to implement when you are.

message.content: Does this plan lead to an actually solid AND elegant solution?

content.text:
Honestly, I think it's solid now — the three review rounds caught real issues (trash without sizes, scanning phase, dead code, a11y). But let me think about whether it's truly *elegant*...

**The component itself is clean.** Pure bar, no layout opinions, two size variants with matching radii. Minimal API. Good.

**The grid layout is the right call.** `auto 1fr auto` is exactly the CSS pattern for this. Labels align, bars align, details align. No fiddly absolute positioning or nested flexbox.

**One thing I'm not fully sure about:** the `{#if bytesTotal > 0}` conditional inside the grid. When the size bar is hidden (trash without sizes), you get a single-bar grid with orphaned columns. The grid still works — CSS grid handles missing rows gracefully — but visually it might look odd to have one bar in a layout designed for two. The label "Items" + one bar + detail would span the 3-column grid correctly, but the "meta" row below a single bar might feel like it has too much vertical space. This is a visual judgment call we'd need to see in practice.

**The rest feels right.** Hiding during scanning avoids ugly empty state. `currentFile` staying visible during scanning is correct for radical transparency. The transition standardization (0.15s) is a reasonable middle ground. No fillColor prop keeps it tight.

I'd say: yes, this is solid and elegant enough to implement confidently. The single-bar-for-trash edge case might need a small CSS tweak once we see it, but the architecture won't need to change.

Want me to start implementing?

Lead a team of Opus agents to deliver on this plan.

You:
- You don't do any work, only oversee the agents!
- You're the leader that keeps this project together, and they do the work. I need your context window to have capacity
  left for post-implementation checks, fixes, thinking, etc.
- Run the agents sequentially, we're in no rush. Unless you predict that the quality is better if they work in parallel.
  And look at what they did between the milestones. Try to use the output of the previous agents as input for the next
  ones.

Agents:
- It's your responsibility that the _whole_ plan gets executed. From time to time, agents skip parts of their part of
  the plan. Give them a clear scope and ask them to do the whole thing. Instruct the agents to thoroughly review their
  work before submitting it to you. They should only say that they're done when they finished all parts of their job.
- Also, agents sometimes do the opposite and don't understand what milestone they ought to complete, and jump on the
  whole plan. This usually results in a disaster in quality because they run out of context, they auto-compress, then
  the compressed agent lacks proper understanding of our values and what we're doing. So again, give them a clear scope.
- Ask every agent to reflect whether they are satisfied with what they'd done. Make them ask: "Is what I've done solid
  AND elegant? You proud and confident about it?" — If the answer is "no" to either, they should adjust, then rinse and
  repeat.
- They should also see if there is something else to fix, like any latent bugs that only need 10–15 LoC changes around
  their part of the development. They're encouraged to fix these. Correctness and bug-free code over crystal-clean
  commits.

Final review:
- In the end, ask +1 Opus agent to do a thorough review of the execution, and flag if anything is skipped, broken,
  incomplete, etc.
- Have +1 Opus agent run `./scripts/check.sh` and make sure that it's green, even if checks fail on unrelated things.
- Do a review yourself, and report: is this something you're proud of? Is this solid AND elegant? Is something missing?
Let me start by reading the plan, then orchestrate the agents sequentially.

content.content:
1→# Dual progress bars for file operations 2→ 3→## Why 4→ 5→The `TransferProgressDialog` shows one byte-based progress bar with text stats below. But file operations have two 6→independent progress dimensions: bytes transferred and files processed. These can diverge significantly — copying one 7→huge file vs many tiny files produces very different bar behaviors. Showing both gives the user a clearer mental model 8→of what's happening (design principle: radical transparency). 9→ 10→The progress bar code is also duplicated — `TransferProgressDialog` and `ProgressOverlay` each have their own inline CSS 11→bars with no shared component. 12→ 13→## What we're building 14→ 15→1. A reusable `ProgressBar.svelte` component (pure visual bar, no layout opinions) 16→2. Dual progress bars in `TransferProgressDialog` (size + file count) 17→3. `ProgressOverlay` refactored to use the shared component 18→ 19→## What we're NOT building 20→ 21→**Rollback progress.** The backend's `CopyTransaction::rollback()` (`state.rs:381`) is synchronous with no event 22→emission. Backwards-animating bars would require backend changes (emitting progress during file deletion). The current 23→spinner + "Deleting N copied files..." is correct for an indeterminate operation. Future work if needed. 24→ 25→## Design decisions 26→ 27→### ProgressBar is just the bar 28→ 29→**Why:** `ProgressOverlay` uses a horizontal row layout (bar + percentage + ETA inline). `TransferProgressDialog` needs a 30→grid layout (label, bar, detail). file.content:
# Dual progress bars for file operations ## Why The `TransferProgressDialog` shows one byte-based progress bar with text stats below. But file operations have two independent progress dimensions: bytes transferred and files processed. These can diverge significantly — copying one huge file vs many tiny files produces very different bar behaviors. Showing both gives the user a clearer mental model of what's happening (design principle: radical transparency). The progress bar code is also duplicated — `TransferProgressDialog` and `ProgressOverlay` each have their own inline CSS bars with no shared component. ## What we're building 1. A reusable `ProgressBar.svelte` component (pure visual bar, no layout opinions) 2. Dual progress bars in `TransferProgressDialog` (size + file count) 3. `ProgressOverlay` refactored to use the shared component ## What we're NOT building **Rollback progress.** The backend's `CopyTransaction::rollback()` (`state.rs:381`) is synchronous with no event emission. Backwards-animating bars would require backend changes (emitting progress during file deletion). The current spinner + "Deleting N copied files..." is correct for an indeterminate operation. Future work if needed. ## Design decisions ### ProgressBar is just the bar **Why:** `ProgressOverlay` uses a horizontal row layout (bar + percentage + ETA inline). `TransferProgressDialog` needs a grid layout (label, bar, detail). These are fundamentally different layouts. If the component includes label/detail props, one consumer won't use them, making the API dishonest. A pure bar component is more composable — consumers arrange labels however they need. ### Size variants instead of pixel props **Why:** The codebase uses design tokens (`--spacing-*`, `--radius-*`, `--font-size-*`) rather than raw values. A `size: 'sm' | 'md'` prop (4px / 8px) is idiomatic. `sm` for `ProgressOverlay`'s compact floating indicator, `md` for dialog-level progress. Border radius scales with size: `sm` uses `--radius-xs` (2px), `md` uses `--radius-sm` (4px) — matching what each consumer had before. ### No fillColor prop The only potential use was rollback styling, which we're not building. Removing it keeps the API minimal and avoids an escape hatch that bypasses the design token system. Can be added later if a real use case arises. ### Inline grid layout for dual bars **Why:** A vertical stack of label-above-bar pairs creates a text/bar/text/bar/text sandwich — visually noisy. An inline grid aligns both bars side by side with labels left and stats right. Compact, scannable, bars are the focal element: ``` Size [========================] 1.2 / 3.5 GB (34%) Files [================== ] 42 / 100 45.2 MB/s · ~2 min remaining ``` CSS grid with `grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto`. Meta row spans all columns. ### Hide progress grid during scanning phase During scanning, both `bytesTotal` and `filesTotal` are 0. Two empty bars at 0% with "0 / 0" labels is ugly and uninformative. The stage indicator already communicates "Scanning" with a spinner. The progress grid is only shown once the active phase begins (when `phase !== 'scanning'`). ### Hide size bar when byte totals are unavailable Trash operations without `itemSizes` (when item sizes aren't known) have `bytesTotal = 0` throughout. A size bar stuck at "0 B / 0 B (0%)" is misleading. Conditionally hide the size row when `bytesTotal === 0` and we're past scanning. ## Implementation ### Step 1: Create `ProgressBar.svelte` **File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressBar.svelte` Props: - `value: number` — 0–1 fractional progress (required) - `size?: 'sm' | 'md'` — bar height + radius. `sm` = 4px / `--radius-xs`, `md` = 8px / `--radius-sm`. Default `'md'` - `ariaLabel?: string` — accessible label for screen readers Internals: - `percent = $derived(Math.min(100, Math.round(value * 100)))` - Track div: `role="progressbar"`, `aria-valuenow={percent}`, `aria-valuemin={0}`, `aria-valuemax={100}`, optional `aria-label={ariaLabel}` - Fill div: `transition: width 0.15s ease-out` (standardized from the existing 0.1s and 0.3s) - Styling: `--color-bg-tertiary` track, `--color-accent` fill Note: This also improves accessibility — the existing `TransferProgressDialog` bar had no `role="progressbar"` or ARIA attributes. ### Step 2: Refactor `ProgressOverlay.svelte` **File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressOverlay.svelte` **Intent:** Validate the new component in the simpler consumer first. - Import and use `` - Remove the inline `.progress-bar` and `.progress-fill` CSS rules - Keep `.progress-row`, `.progress-text`, `.progress-eta` (the surrounding layout is unchanged) ### Step 3: Dual bars in `TransferProgressDialog.svelte` **File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/transfer/TransferProgressDialog.svelte` Script: - Import `ProgressBar` - Remove `percentComplete` derived (line 189) — no longer used, replaced by inline expressions Template — replace lines 799–828 (progress section + stats section) with a CSS grid. Preserve the current file indicator (lines 830–835) after the grid — it should remain outside the `{#if}` since the backend emits `current_file` during scanning too, and showing it supports radical transparency. Only show the bars when past scanning: ```svelte {#if phase !== 'scanning'}
{#if bytesTotal > 0} Size {formatBytes(bytesDone)} / {formatBytes(bytesTotal)} ({Math.round((bytesDone / bytesTotal) * 100)}%) {/if} {operationType === 'trash' ? 'Items' : 'Files'} 0 ? filesDone / filesTotal : 0} ariaLabel="File progress" /> {filesDone} / {filesTotal}
{#if stats.bytesPerSecond > 0} {formatBytes(stats.bytesPerSecond)}/s {/if} {#if stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining !== null} ~{formatDuration(stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining)} remaining {/if}
{/if} ``` CSS: - `.progress-grid`: `display: grid; grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto; gap: var(--spacing-xs) var(--spacing-sm); align-items: center; padding: 0 var(--spacing-xl); margin-bottom: var(--spacing-md)` - `.progress-label`: `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-text-tertiary)` - `.progress-detail`: `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-text-secondary); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; text-align: right` - `.progress-meta`: `grid-column: 1 / -1; display: flex; justify-content: space-between; font-size: var(--font-size-sm)` - `.progress-speed`: `color: var(--color-text-secondary); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` - `.progress-eta`: `color: var(--color-text-tertiary)` - Remove old: `.progress-section`, `.progress-bar-container`, `.progress-bar`, `.progress-info`, `.progress-percent`, `.eta`, `.stats-section`, `.stat-row`, `.stat-label`, `.stat-value` ### Step 4: Update docs **`apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/CLAUDE.md`** — add ProgressBar section following the existing format (see ProgressOverlay section for the pattern). Props table with types and notes. Mention consumers (ProgressOverlay, TransferProgressDialog). **`apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/CLAUDE.md`** — in the TransferProgressDialog bullet, update "Progress bar with ETA, speed (MB/s), current file" → "Dual progress bars (size + file count) with speed, ETA, current file" ### Step 5: Checks - `./scripts/check.sh --svelte` (typecheck, lint, stylelint, format) ## Testing - Manual: `pnpm dev` → copy a large folder → verify two bars animate independently - Manual: delete files → both bars progress (backend tracks bytes for delete/trash too) - Manual: trash files → "Items" label appears instead of "Files" - Manual: cancel a copy → rollback spinner still works unchanged - Manual: trigger indexing → ProgressOverlay bar still works - Manual: start an operation → verify scanning phase shows stages but no empty bars - Automated: existing vitest + checks should pass (no behavior changes, just UI restructuring)

content.text:
Good. Three milestones for the agents:

1. **Agent 1:** Create `ProgressBar.svelte` + refactor `ProgressOverlay.svelte` (steps 1–2)
2. **Agent 2:** Dual bars in `TransferProgressDialog.svelte` (step 3)
3. **Agent 3:** Update docs (step 4)
4. **Agent 4:** Final review
5. **Agent 5:** Run checks

Starting with Agent 1.

You are implementing steps 1 and 2 of the dual progress bars plan. Your scope is ONLY these two steps — do not touch TransferProgressDialog or any other files beyond what's listed below.

## Context

Read these files first:
- `docs/specs/dual-progress-bars-plan.md` — the full plan (your scope is steps 1 and 2 only)
- `docs/style-guide.md` — writing and code conventions
- `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressOverlay.svelte` — the file you'll refactor
- `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/CLAUDE.md` — for component conventions and patterns

## Your tasks

### Task 1: Create `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressBar.svelte`

A pure visual progress bar component. Read the plan for exact props and behavior:
- `value: number` (0–1)
- `size?: 'sm' | 'md'` (default 'md'). `sm` = 4px height + `--radius-xs`, `md` = 8px height + `--radius-sm`
- `ariaLabel?: string`

The component is JUST the bar — track + fill + ARIA. No labels, no layout opinions. ~30 lines total.
Use `$props()` and `$derived()` (Svelte 5). Functional only, no classes.
All CSS must use design tokens from `app.css` — no raw hex values. Use `var(--color-bg-tertiary)` for track, `var(--color-accent)` for fill, appropriate radius tokens.

### Task 2: Refactor `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressOverlay.svelte`

- Import and use `<ProgressBar value={progress ?? 0} size="sm" />`
- Remove the inline `.progress-bar` and `.progress-fill` CSS rules (they're now in the ProgressBar component)
- Keep `.progress-row`, `.progress-text`, `.progress-eta` (the surrounding layout is unchanged)
- The behavior and appearance should be identical to before — this is a pure refactor

## Quality check

Before you finish, ask yourself: "Is what I've done solid AND elegant? Am I proud and confident about it?"
- Is the component API minimal and clean?
- Does ProgressOverlay look/behave exactly the same as before?
- Are all CSS tokens valid (check app.css if unsure)?
- Is the code idiomatic Svelte 5?

If the answer is "no" to any of these, adjust before finishing. Do the COMPLETE scope — both tasks, no skipping.

1→# UI primitives 2→ 3→Reusable UI components used across the entire desktop app. 4→ 5→## Key files 6→ 7→| File | Purpose | 8→| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | 9→| `ModalDialog.svelte` | Central modal container: overlay, dragging, Escape, focus, MCP tracking | 10→| `dialog-registry.ts` | `SOFT_DIALOG_REGISTRY` array — single source of truth for all dialog IDs | 11→| `Button.svelte` | Styled button with variant and size props | 12→| `CommandBox.svelte` | Copyable terminal command (monospace + Copy button) | 13→| `LoadingIcon.svelte` | Animated spinner with progressive status text | 14→| `AlertDialog.svelte` | Single-action confirmation dialog built on `ModalDialog` | 15→| `ProgressOverlay.svelte` | Floating top-right progress indicator: spinner, progress bar, ETA | 16→| `toast/` | Centralized toast notification system — store, container, item | 17→ 18→## ModalDialog 19→ 20→Props: 21→ 22→| Prop | Type | Notes | 23→| ---------------- | ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | 24→| `titleId` | `string` | Used for `aria-labelledby` | 25→| `title` | Snippet | Rendered as `

` in the title bar | 26→| `children` | Snippet | Dialog body | 27→| `dialogId` | `SoftDialogId?` | Auto-calls `notifyDialogOpened`/`notifyDialogClosed` on mount/destroy | 28→| `onclose` | `() => void`? | Renders × button; also called on Escape | 29→| `draggable` | `boolean` | Default `true`. Title bar drag moves the dialog. | 30→| `blur` | `boolean` | `true` → 0.6 opacity + `backdrop-filter: blur(4px)` overlay | 31→| `containerStyle` | `string` | Inline style appended to the dialog element (for sizing, colors) | 32→| `role` | `'dialog'` \| `'alertdialog'` | Default `'dialog'` | 33→ 34→The overlay element receives `tabindex="-1"` and is focused on mount so Escape/keydown events are captured without a 35→visible focus ring on the scrim. 36→ 37→## Dialog registry 38→ 39→`dialog-registry.ts` exports `SOFT_DIALOG_REGISTRY` (a `const` array) and the derived `SoftDialogId` union type. Using a 40→`dialogId` not in the registry produces a TypeScript error. The registry is sent to the Rust backend at startup so the 41→MCP "available dialogs" resource stays in sync. 42→ 43→To add a new dialog: 44→ 45→1. Add an entry to `SOFT_DIALOG_REGISTRY` in `dialog-registry.ts`. 46→2. Pass the new id as `dialogId` to `ModalDialog` — MCP tracking is then automatic. 47→ 48→## Tooltip (`../tooltip/tooltip.ts`) 49→ 50→Global tooltip system via Svelte action. Apple-style frosted glass appearance, 400ms show delay, auto-flips above/below 51→viewport. 52→ 53→Usage: 54→ 55→```svelte 56→import { tooltip } from '$lib/tooltip/tooltip' 57→ 58→ 59→... 60→ 61→ 62→Line two' }}>... 63→ 64→ 65→... 66→ 67→ 68→... 69→ 70→ 71→... 72→``` 73→ 74→`TooltipParam` type: `string | { text?, html?, shortcut?, overflowOnly? } | null | undefined`. 75→ 76→The tooltip element has `white-space: pre-line` and uses global CSS classes, so `` etc. work 77→inside `{ html }` tooltips. The `html` variant renders via `innerHTML` — only use with trusted content. 78→ 79→## Button 80→ 81→Variants: `primary` | `secondary` (default) | `danger`. Sizes: `regular` (default) | `mini`. Extends 82→`HTMLButtonAttributes` so all native button attributes pass through. 83→ 84→## LoadingIcon 85→ 86→Progressive status text driven by props (mutually exclusive, evaluated top-down): 87→ 88→1. `finalizingCount` set → "All N file/files loaded. Sorting your files, preparing view..." 89→2. `loadedCount` set → "Loaded N file/files..." 90→3. `openingFolder` true → "Opening folder..." 91→4. Default → "Loading..." 92→ 93→`showCancelHint` adds "Press ESC to cancel and go back" below the spinner. The container uses a 400ms `fadeIn` animation 94→where the first 50% is invisible (effectively 200ms before fade begins), avoiding flash for fast loads. 95→ 96→## ProgressOverlay 97→ 98→Floating top-right overlay for showing progress on long-running operations. Uses `pointer-events: none` so it never 99→blocks clicks. Two layout modes: 100→ 101→- **Label only** (`progress` omitted): Spinner + single-line label. Compact layout. 102→- **With progress** (`progress` passed, even as `null`): Spinner + column layout with label, optional detail text, 103→ optional progress bar + percentage + ETA. The column has `min-width: 160px` to give the progress bar enough room. 104→ 105→Props: 106→ 107→| Prop | Type | Notes | 108→| ---------- | ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 109→| `visible` | `boolean` | Show/hide the overlay | 110→| `label` | `string` | Main text (for example, "Scanning...", "Computing directory sizes...") | 111→| `detail` | `string?` | Secondary text (for example, "42,000 entries") | 112→| `progress` | `number \| null` | 0–1 for determinate bar, `null` for no bar. Omit entirely for compact layout. | 113→| `eta` | `string \| null` | Pre-formatted ETA string (for example, "~2 min left") | 114→ 115→Used by `ScanStatusOverlay` (indexing progress). Designed to also be used for replay progress. 116→ 117→## Toast system (`toast/`) 118→ 119→Centralized toast notifications with stacking, levels, and two dismissal modes. 120→ 121→- **Store** (`toast-store.svelte.ts`): Module-level `$state` array. `addToast(content, options?)` accepts a `Snippet` or 122→ plain `string`. Optional `id` for dedup (replace in place). Max 5 visible. 123→- **Container** (`ToastContainer.svelte`): Mounted once in `(main)/+layout.svelte`. Fixed top-right, stacks vertically. 124→- **Item** (`ToastItem.svelte`): Frame, close button, auto-dismiss timer for transient toasts. 125→ 126→Levels: `info` (default), `success`, `warn`, `error`. Dismissal: `transient` (4s timeout + nav-dismiss, default) or 127→`persistent`. 128→ 129→Call `dismissTransientToasts()` on pane navigation to clear stale feedback. 130→ 131→## CommandBox 132→ 133→`CommandBox.svelte` — monospace terminal command with a one-click Copy button and 2-second "Copied!" feedback. Takes a 134→single `command` string prop. Handles clipboard internally (`copyToClipboard` with `navigator.clipboard` fallback). 135→Parent controls spacing via its own wrapper. 1→# Style guide
     2→
     3→Writing, code, and design styles.
     4→
     5→## Writing
     6→
     7→- Wording
     8→    - **Use a friendly style**: Make all texts informal, friendly, encouraging, and concise.
     9→    - **Use active voice**: Always prefer active voice. "We moved your files" not "Your files were moved." This is
    10→      especially important for success messages, error messages, and UI copy. Passive voice creeps in easily. Watch for
    11→      it.
    12→    - **Abbreviate English**: Use "I'm", "don't", and such.
    13→    - **Don't trivialize**: Avoid terminology of "just", "simple", "easy", and "all you have to do".
    14→    - **Use gender-neutral language**: Use they/them rather than he/him/she/her. Use "folks" or "everyone" rather than
    15→      "guys".
    16→    - **Use universally understood terms**: Use "start" instead of "kickoff", and "end" instead of "wrap up".
    17→    - **Avoid ableist language**: "placeholder value" rather than "dummy value". No "lame", "sanity check" which derive
    18→      from disabilities.
    19→    - **Avoid violent terms**: "stop a process" rather than "kill" or "nuke" it.
    20→    - **Avoid exclusionary terminology**: Prefer "primary/secondary" or "main/replica" over "master/slave". Use
    21→      "allowlist/denylist" over "whitelist/blacklist".
    22→    - **Use verbs, not verb-noun phrases**: "Search" not "Make a search". "Save" not "Perform a save".
    23→    - **Don't use permissive language**: Give users confidence. "Add repos and start searching" not "Add repos and you
    24→      can start searching."
    25→    - **Be mindful of user expertise**: Avoid jargon. Link to definitions and explain concepts when necessary.
    26→    - **Avoid latinisms**: For example, use "for example" instead of "e.g.".
    27→    - **Avoid abbreviations**: Very common acronyms like "URL" are okay.
    28→    - **Some casual terms are okay**: Use "docs", not "documentation". Use "dev" for developer and "gen" for generation
    29→      where appropriate and understandable.
    30→- Punctuation, capitalization, numbers
    31→    - **Use sentence case in titles**: Regardless whether visible on the UI or dev only.
    32→    - **Use sentence case in labels**: Applies to buttons, labels, and similar. But omit periods on short microcopy.
    33→    - **Capitalize names correctly**: For example, there is GitHub but mailcow.
    34→    - **Use the Oxford comma**: Use "1, 2, and 3" rather than "1, 2 and 3".
    35→    - **Use en dashes but no em dashes**: en dash for ranges, but avoid structures that'd need an em dash.
    36→    - **Use colon for lists**: Use the format I used in this list you're reading right now.
    37→    - **Spell out numbers one through nine.** Use numerals for 10+.
    38→    - **Use ISO dates**: Use YYYY-MM-DD wherever it makes sense.
    39→- UI
    40→    - **Error messages**: Keep conversational, positive, actionable, and specific. Never use the words "error" or
    41→      "failed" — we wouldn't say those in conversation. Suggest a next step.
    42→        - "Couldn't rename the file. Try again?" not "Error: Rename operation failed."
    43→        - "Password must contain at least 12 characters" not "Password format is invalid (minimum 12 characters)"
    44→        - "Sorry, we couldn't save your changes. Try again?" not "Failed to save changes."
    45→    - **Success messages**: Talk about the user, not the action. Make success implicit and warm.
    46→        - "Your files moved to ~/Documents" not "Move operation completed successfully."
    47→        - "Shortcut saved — it's ready to use" not "Shortcut successfully created."
    48→    - **Confirmation dialogs**: Title = `verb + noun` question. Body = plain irreversibility warning. Buttons = outcome
    49→      verbs, never "Yes / No".
    50→        - "Delete 3 files?" / "This can't be undone" / **Cancel** · **Delete**
    51→        - "Discard unsaved changes?" / **Cancel** · **Discard**
    52→    - **Empty states**: Say what belongs here and offer a next step. Empty states reveal potential, not absence.
    53→        - "Your bookmarks will appear here. Create your first bookmark!" not "No bookmarks found."
    54→    - **Link the destination, not the sentence**: In sentences, only link the text that describes where you'll go.
    55→        - "Learn how to [set up shortcuts]" not "[Learn how to set up shortcuts]."
    56→    - **Helper text**: Only add if users actually need it. More messages = more intimidating. Keep it short.
    57→        - "8–12 characters" not "Password must be between 8–12 characters"
    58→    - **Start UI actions with a verb**: This makes buttons and links more actionable. Use "Create user" instead of "New
    59→      user".
    60→    - **Give examples in placeholder text**: Use "Example: 2025-01-01" or "name@example.com" rather than an instruction
    61→      like "Enter your email".
    62→    - **Never write "something(s)"**: Always pluralize dynamically: "1 user" instead of "1 user(s)".
    63→- Specific terms
    64→    - **Folder vs directory**: We know these mean the same. We allow both. Use whichever feels better in each situation.
    65→      Like, on the backend, listing "folders" with `readdir` feels wrong, but also, "folder" comes more natural on the
    66→      front-end and end-user docs.
    67→
    68→## Code
    69→
    70→### Comments
    71→
    72→Only add JSDoc that actually adds info. No tautologies.
    73→
    74→- ✅ Add meaningful comments for public functions, methods, and types to help the next dev.
    75→- ❌ BUT DO NOT use JSDoc for stuff like `Gets the name` for a function called `getName` :D
    76→- ⚠️ Before adding JSDoc, try using a more descriptive name for the function/param/variable.
    77→- ❌ DO NOT repeat TypeScript types in `@param`/`@returns`.
    78→- ✅ USE JSDoc to mark caveats, tricky/unusual solutions, formats (`YYYY-MM-DD`), and constraints (`must end with /`)
    79→
    80→### TypeScript
    81→
    82→- Only functional components and modules. No classes.
    83→- Don't use `any` type. ESLint will error.
    84→- Prefer functional programming (map, reduce, some, forEach) and pure functions wherever it makes sense.
    85→- Use `const` for everything, unless it makes the code unnecessarily verbose.
    86→- Start function names with a verb, unless unidiomatic in the specific case.
    87→- Use `camelCase` for variable and constant names, including module-level constants.
    88→- Put constants closest to where they are used. If only used in one function, put it in that function.
    89→- For maps, try to name them like `somethingToSomethingElseMap`. That avoids unnecessary comments.
    90→- Keep interfaces minimal: only export what you must export.
    91→
    92→### Svelte 5
    93→
    94→- `$state()` can only live in `.svelte` or `.svelte.ts` files, not plain `.ts`.
    95→- Template arrow function closures need explicit type annotations to avoid `any` args from Svelte's event system.
    96→- When extracting logic from `.svelte` to `.ts`, use callback-based deps (getters) rather than threading reactive state.
    97→
    98→### Rust
    99→
   100→- Max 120 char lines, 4-space indent, cognitive complexity threshold: 15, enforced by clippy.
   101→
   102→### CSS
   103→
   104→- `html { font-size: 16px; }` is set so `1rem = 16px`. Use `px` by default but can use `rem` if it's more descriptive.
   105→- Use variables for colors, spacing, and the such, in `app.css`.
   106→- Always think about accessibility when designing, and dark + light modes.
   107→- For the full design system (color tokens, typography scale, spacing, component patterns), see
   108→  [design-system.md](design-system.md).
   109→
   110→## Design
   111→
   112→See [design-principles.md](design-principles.md) for product design values (UX, accessibility, cancellation, platform
   113→behavior). Read it when designing features or making UX decisions.
   114→

1→# Dual progress bars for file operations 2→ 3→## Why 4→ 5→The `TransferProgressDialog` shows one byte-based progress bar with text stats below. But file operations have two 6→independent progress dimensions: bytes transferred and files processed. These can diverge significantly — copying one 7→huge file vs many tiny files produces very different bar behaviors. Showing both gives the user a clearer mental model 8→of what's happening (design principle: radical transparency). 9→ 10→The progress bar code is also duplicated — `TransferProgressDialog` and `ProgressOverlay` each have their own inline CSS 11→bars with no shared component. 12→ 13→## What we're building 14→ 15→1. A reusable `ProgressBar.svelte` component (pure visual bar, no layout opinions) 16→2. Dual progress bars in `TransferProgressDialog` (size + file count) 17→3. `ProgressOverlay` refactored to use the shared component 18→ 19→## What we're NOT building 20→ 21→**Rollback progress.** The backend's `CopyTransaction::rollback()` (`state.rs:381`) is synchronous with no event 22→emission. Backwards-animating bars would require backend changes (emitting progress during file deletion). The current 23→spinner + "Deleting N copied files..." is correct for an indeterminate operation. Future work if needed. 24→ 25→## Design decisions 26→ 27→### ProgressBar is just the bar 28→ 29→**Why:** `ProgressOverlay` uses a horizontal row layout (bar + percentage + ETA inline). `TransferProgressDialog` needs a 30→grid layout (label, bar, detail). These are fundamentally different layouts. If the component includes label/detail props, 31→one consumer won't use them, making the API dishonest. A pure bar component is more composable — consumers arrange labels 32→however they need. 33→ 34→### Size variants instead of pixel props 35→ 36→**Why:** The codebase uses design tokens (`--spacing-*`, `--radius-*`, `--font-size-*`) rather than raw values. A 37→`size: 'sm' | 'md'` prop (4px / 8px) is idiomatic. `sm` for `ProgressOverlay`'s compact floating indicator, `md` for 38→dialog-level progress. Border radius scales with size: `sm` uses `--radius-xs` (2px), `md` uses `--radius-sm` (4px) — 39→matching what each consumer had before. 40→ 41→### No fillColor prop 42→ 43→The only potential use was rollback styling, which we're not building. Removing it keeps the API minimal and avoids 44→an escape hatch that bypasses the design token system. Can be added later if a real use case arises. 45→ 46→### Inline grid layout for dual bars 47→ 48→**Why:** A vertical stack of label-above-bar pairs creates a text/bar/text/bar/text sandwich — visually noisy. An inline 49→grid aligns both bars side by side with labels left and stats right. Compact, scannable, bars are the focal element: 50→ 51→``` 52→Size [========================] 1.2 / 3.5 GB (34%) 53→Files [================== ] 42 / 100 54→ 45.2 MB/s · ~2 min remaining 55→``` 56→ 57→CSS grid with `grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto`. Meta row spans all columns. 58→ 59→### Hide progress grid during scanning phase 60→ 61→During scanning, both `bytesTotal` and `filesTotal` are 0. Two empty bars at 0% with "0 / 0" labels is ugly and 62→uninformative. The stage indicator already communicates "Scanning" with a spinner. The progress grid is only shown once 63→the active phase begins (when `phase !== 'scanning'`). 64→ 65→### Hide size bar when byte totals are unavailable 66→ 67→Trash operations without `itemSizes` (when item sizes aren't known) have `bytesTotal = 0` throughout. A size bar stuck 68→at "0 B / 0 B (0%)" is misleading. Conditionally hide the size row when `bytesTotal === 0` and we're past scanning. 69→ 70→## Implementation 71→ 72→### Step 1: Create `ProgressBar.svelte` 73→ 74→**File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressBar.svelte` 75→ 76→Props: 77→- `value: number` — 0–1 fractional progress (required) 78→- `size?: 'sm' | 'md'` — bar height + radius. `sm` = 4px / `--radius-xs`, `md` = 8px / `--radius-sm`. Default `'md'` 79→- `ariaLabel?: string` — accessible label for screen readers 80→ 81→Internals: 82→- `percent = $derived(Math.min(100, Math.round(value * 100)))` 83→- Track div: `role="progressbar"`, `aria-valuenow={percent}`, `aria-valuemin={0}`, `aria-valuemax={100}`, 84→ optional `aria-label={ariaLabel}` 85→- Fill div: `transition: width 0.15s ease-out` (standardized from the existing 0.1s and 0.3s) 86→- Styling: `--color-bg-tertiary` track, `--color-accent` fill 87→ 88→Note: This also improves accessibility — the existing `TransferProgressDialog` bar had no `role="progressbar"` or ARIA 89→attributes. 90→ 91→### Step 2: Refactor `ProgressOverlay.svelte` 92→ 93→**File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/ProgressOverlay.svelte` 94→ 95→**Intent:** Validate the new component in the simpler consumer first. 96→ 97→- Import and use `` 98→- Remove the inline `.progress-bar` and `.progress-fill` CSS rules 99→- Keep `.progress-row`, `.progress-text`, `.progress-eta` (the surrounding layout is unchanged) 100→ 101→### Step 3: Dual bars in `TransferProgressDialog.svelte` 102→ 103→**File:** `apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/transfer/TransferProgressDialog.svelte` 104→ 105→Script: 106→- Import `ProgressBar` 107→- Remove `percentComplete` derived (line 189) — no longer used, replaced by inline expressions 108→ 109→Template — replace lines 799–828 (progress section + stats section) with a CSS grid. Preserve the current file 110→indicator (lines 830–835) after the grid — it should remain outside the `{#if}` since the backend emits `current_file` 111→during scanning too, and showing it supports radical transparency. Only show the bars when past scanning: 112→ 113→```svelte 114→{#if phase !== 'scanning'} 115→
116→ {#if bytesTotal > 0} 117→ Size 118→ 119→ 120→ {formatBytes(bytesDone)} / {formatBytes(bytesTotal)} 121→ ({Math.round((bytesDone / bytesTotal) * 100)}%) 122→ 123→ {/if} 124→ 125→ {operationType === 'trash' ? 'Items' : 'Files'} 126→ 0 ? filesDone / filesTotal : 0} ariaLabel="File progress" /> 127→ {filesDone} / {filesTotal} 128→ 129→
130→ {#if stats.bytesPerSecond > 0} 131→ {formatBytes(stats.bytesPerSecond)}/s 132→ {/if} 133→ {#if stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining !== null} 134→ ~{formatDuration(stats.estimatedSecondsRemaining)} remaining 135→ {/if} 136→
137→
138→{/if} 139→``` 140→ 141→CSS: 142→- `.progress-grid`: `display: grid; grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto; gap: var(--spacing-xs) var(--spacing-sm); 143→ align-items: center; padding: 0 var(--spacing-xl); margin-bottom: var(--spacing-md)` 144→- `.progress-label`: `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-text-tertiary)` 145→- `.progress-detail`: `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-text-secondary); 146→ font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; text-align: right` 147→- `.progress-meta`: `grid-column: 1 / -1; display: flex; justify-content: space-between; 148→ font-size: var(--font-size-sm)` 149→- `.progress-speed`: `color: var(--color-text-secondary); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` 150→- `.progress-eta`: `color: var(--color-text-tertiary)` 151→- Remove old: `.progress-section`, `.progress-bar-container`, `.progress-bar`, `.progress-info`, `.progress-percent`, 152→ `.eta`, `.stats-section`, `.stat-row`, `.stat-label`, `.stat-value` 153→ 154→### Step 4: Update docs 155→ 156→**`apps/desktop/src/lib/ui/CLAUDE.md`** — add ProgressBar section following the existing format (see ProgressOverlay 157→section for the pattern). Props table with types and notes. Mention consumers (ProgressOverlay, TransferProgressDialog). 158→ 159→**`apps/desktop/src/lib/file-operations/CLAUDE.md`** — in the TransferProgressDialog bullet, update 160→"Progress bar with ETA, speed (MB/s), current file" → "Dual progress bars (size + file count) with speed, ETA, current 161→file" 162→ 163→### Step 5: Checks 164→ 165→- `./scripts/check.sh --svelte` (typecheck, lint, stylelint, format) 166→ 167→## Testing 168→ 169→- Manual: `pnpm dev` → copy a large folder → verify two bars animate independently 170→- Manual: delete files → both bars progress (backend tracks bytes for delete/trash too) 171→- Manual: trash files → "Items" label appears instead of "Files" 172→- Manual: cancel a copy → rollback spinner still works unchanged 173→- Manual: trigger indexing → ProgressOverlay bar still works 174→- Manual: start an operation → verify scanning phase shows stages but no empty bars 175→- Automated: existing vitest + checks should pass (no behavior changes, just UI restructuring) 176→

     2→    import { onMount, onDestroy } from 'svelte'
     3→    import {
     4→        copyFiles,
     5→        copyBetweenVolumes,
     6→        moveFiles,
     7→        deleteFiles,

    10→        onWriteComplete,

    18→        DEFAULT_VOLUME_ID,
    19→        type WriteProgressEvent,

    25→    } from '$lib/tauri-commands'
    26→    import type {
    27→        TransferOperationType,
    28→        WriteOperationPhase,
